title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Ownership, Access Points, and Public Access · Execution Trace: Prefix Tenant Isolation
summary: A bucket is private by convention, one application role has s3:*, tenant prefixes are interpolated from user input, and ACLs conflict with ownership and public-block assumptions. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prefix tenant isolation increment in least-privilege S3 authorization proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Prefix Tenant Isolation · Theory Prefix Tenant Isolation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 4789 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
